profile.name: Elizabeth
profile.role_in_household: Primary client and meeting driver; tends to coordinate accounts, deadlines, and the family’s planning priorities.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate-to-high: understands taxable vs retirement accounts, diversification, and basic tax impacts; may not recall provider/product details without statements.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate in behavior: willing to take market risk for growth but wants guardrails (cash buffer, rebalancing rules) and gets uneasy during sharp drawdowns.
profile.communication_style: Structured and direct; asks pointed questions. Uses hedging and imperfect recall when unsure (“I think…”, “roughly…”, “I don’t remember exactly”). Will interrupt to clarify numbers and confirm next steps.
emotional_tendencies: anxious when cash flow feels tight
profile.decision_making_style: Analytical and plan-based: wants tradeoffs (taxes/fees/liquidity) explained, then prefers to decide after a brief pause unless it’s an obvious win.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Cautiously open: trust increases with accuracy, transparency, and follow-through on action items.
hidden_concerns: balancing retirement saving with three kids and near-term needs
making a costly tax mistake in the taxable account

profile.name: Danie
profile.role_in_household: Spouse/partner; contributes perspective on long-term security and is attached to existing choices, but often lets Elizabeth steer the agenda unless he feels challenged.
profile.financial_literacy_level: Moderate: understands budgeting and long-term investing at a high level; less comfortable with account mechanics and paperwork, and tends not to remember exact figures on the spot.
profile.risk_attitude_behavioral: Moderate but more tolerant of complexity/illiquidity: comfortable holding non-traditional investments if the rationale feels sound; dislikes being pushed to change quickly and may dig in if he feels judged.
profile.communication_style: Conversational and indirect; uses fillers and ranges (“uh…”, “kind of…”, “around…”). Often defers, then circles back with a concern or asks for reassurance.
profile.decision_making_style: Consensus-seeking: wants alignment with Elizabeth; prefers a simple recommendation with a clear rationale and minimal ongoing maintenance.
profile.trust_level_toward_advisor: Neutral-to-guarded initially: warms up if the advisor listens, avoids jargon, and doesn’t sound salesy.
hidden_concerns: worries about health and work stamina through retirement years
concerned about liquidity for surprises but reluctant to admit it
